title: mfa — declared entitlements
description: Five entitlement keys. TOTP + recovery codes are baseline (every tier — this is the security floor Academorix enforces). WebAuthn, multiple devices, and step-up are progressive tier upgrades. Entitlements are enforced at the HTTP surface by `entitlement.enforce:<key>` middleware; declared here because they are properties of the CHALLENGE / ENROLMENT protocols this module owns. Some overlap with identity/identity's `mfa_totp` / `mfa_webauthn` / `mfa_recovery_codes` — those are declared on identity/identity because they gate SECRET STORAGE (can the Identity carry an encrypted secret at rest); the keys below are declared here because they gate PROTOCOL ACCESS (can the caller invoke the enrolment / verification / recovery endpoints).

entitlements.description: Enables the TOTP enrolment endpoints (POST /me/mfa/totp/enroll/preview + /enroll). Baseline across every plan tier because TOTP is the security floor. Redundant with identity/identity's `mfa_totp` — both must be present. Duplicated intentionally so a tenant misconfiguration on one side doesn't accidentally leave the other side open.

Enables the WebAuthn enrolment endpoints (POST /me/mfa/webauthn/register/options + /register). Free tier is gated off to keep the fallback surface simple; team+ can enrol hardware keys. Redundant with identity/identity's `mfa_webauthn`.

Enables the recovery-code endpoints (POST /me/mfa/recovery-codes/regenerate + /download). Always on — without recovery codes, losing the primary factor locks the Identity out. Ten codes generated at enrolment; regenerable on demand.

Enables registering more than one WebAuthn credential per Identity. Free tier caps at 1 credential (encouraging a single phone-based passkey); medium+ tiers allow the full `mfa.webauthn.max_credentials_per_identity` cap (default 10). The observer refuses `webauthn_credentials` inserts when this entitlement is off AND the caller already has a registered credential — with error `WEBAUTHN_MULTIPLE_DEVICES_NOT_ENTITLED` (402).

Enables the `mfa.require_recent_challenge` middleware. Sensitive actions (password change, MFA disable, credential delete, recovery-code regeneration) can require a fresh MFA challenge at the action time. Enterprise-only because it's a friction-adding security feature — most tenants prefer once-per-session MFA; regulated-industry tenants (SOC 2, PCI DSS) opt in for the tighter posture. When the entitlement is off, `mfa.require_recent_challenge` middleware degrades to a pass-through (session-level enforcement via `mfa.require_challenge_complete` still applies).
